input.headline: Are you limiting the time you spend online? We’d like to hear from you

raw.headline: Are you limiting the time you spend online? We’d like to hear from you

winner: Why don't online-averse people ever play hide and seek? Because they always come out after a minute!

candidates.text: Why did the internet break up with its user? It realized they were spending too much time together.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with a clear connection to the headline, concise and specific joke."}

candidates.text: Why did the internet break up with its user? Because it felt smothered by all the constant chatting and surfing around the clock. It needed some time apart to breathe.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, uses personification for humor, and ties back to the headline's theme of limiting online time. It’s slightly generic but still effective and surprising."}

candidates.text: Why don't online platforms ever feel lonely when we're offline? Because they've got plenty of friends... in our browsing history!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, uses a clever twist with 'browsing history' as friends, and ties well to the headline's theme."}

candidates.text: Well, I've been trying to limit my time online... But the internet keeps coming back every time I log out.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, uses a relatable situation with a surprising twist, and plays on the idea of the internet being persistent."}

candidates.text: Why don't online-averse people ever play hide and seek? Because they always come out after a minute!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat related to the headline but lacks strong surprise and specificity. The pun on 'come out after a minute' is weak and not clearly tied to limiting online time."}
